DIVERGE region code: first byte differs at offset 0x17 (guest 0x10017) -- 00 vs 01

Status: WipEout reaches the FirstRsxWrite checkpoint at PPU step
45,690 (deterministic across two bench-boot runs,
`agreement: 0.59%`). The earlier "stuck at MaxSteps" frontier
documented in the prior fixture is gone -- the SWAR-strlen +
label-emission loop the title spun in is no longer the gating
issue; the title now reaches the same FirstRsxWrite checkpoint it
hit before the rldic / rldimi decoder fix invalidated the prior
spurious 20,569-step trip.

The reported divergence is at offset 0x17 of the code region:
the low byte of the ELF header's `e_version` field. CellGov reads
0x00, RPCS3 reads 0x01. Same class as SSHD's 0x35 byte (e_ehsize)
and the historical flOw-side divergences: the header bytes
captured by CellGov's loaded image differ from the in-memory
SELF bytes RPCS3 mmaps. Neither side reads these bytes during
execution; the divergence is non-semantic.

`compare-observations` stops at the first divergence, so the
data region's HLE OPD layout differences (previously documented
across the OPD slot table) are not re-confirmed by this run. The
OPD layout class is shared with SSHD and flOw; classification
remains "PS3-wide, non-semantic".

## History (kept for context)

An earlier CellGov release tripped the `FirstRsxWrite` checkpoint
on WipEout at step 20,569. That trip was spurious -- the PPU
decoder was lumping `rldic` (XO=2) and `rldimi` (XO=3) under the
`rldicl` execution path, and a `rldimi rt, rs, 32, 0` mis-decoded
to clear-rather-than-merge. PS3 code uses this idiom to splice a
32-bit value into the upper half of a 64-bit pointer; under the
mis-decode the upper half was zeroed, producing a pointer whose
later store landed on RSX MMIO at 0xC0000040 by accident,
tripping FirstRsxWrite prematurely.

With the decoder fixed (`rldic` and `rldimi` decoded with correct
mask semantics), WipEout's boot ran past 20,569 and (per the
prior fixture) was observed to stall in a SWAR-strlen +
label-emission loop, never reaching FirstRsxWrite within a 100M-
or 1B-instruction window. That stall is also gone in the current
build: the title reaches the legitimate FirstRsxWrite at 45,690
steps without further decoder or scheduler work, so the prior
"PRX module loading" frontier is no longer the gating issue for
this checkpoint.

## Classes of divergence the prior report documented

Kept here as historical context. These were all classified
non-semantic; the current 0x17 e_version divergence is the same
class as the first item:

- ELF header metadata (code region, 2 bytes): `e_version` and
  the high half of `e_entry` differ because
  `cellgov_firmware decrypt-self` and RPCS3's in-memory SELF
  decryption reconstruct these header bytes differently. Neither
  side reads them during execution.
- SYS_PROC_PARAM block (code region, 12 bytes): CellGov reads the
  PT_PROC supplemental header through the loader but does not
  write the parsed struct back into guest memory; RPCS3 keeps the
  SELF bytes in place. Both runners use the values via internal
  state.
- HLE OPD slots (data region, 960 bytes): CellGov and RPCS3
  assign HLE trampoline stub slot indices in the same module
  order but iterate within a module differently (sorted vs
  declaration order), producing per-slot pointer differences. The
  guest calls through either side's OPDs identically.

Verdict: not strictly equivalent at the byte level. The diverging
header byte at 0x17 is not read by the executing code;
classification remains "PS3-wide, non-semantic". A future cleanup
should re-classify by walking past the ELF-header divergence and
confirming that the only remaining differences are the documented
OPD layout class.

Re-confirmed at the current branch tip: step count is 45,690
(deterministic across runs). Single-PRX bench-boot anchor; the
firmware-set boot path lands at 45,770 because liblv2 and the
13 other foundation PRXs each run their own `module_start` and
issue additional LV2 syscalls that the single-PRX path skips.
Both numbers are deterministic and the divergence-class verdict
above is mode-independent.
